Location & Data

Most Popular Baby Names in Texas

Texas baby name trends featuring the Lone Star State's unique blend of traditional, Hispanic, and modern naming patterns from the latest SSA data.

By Ziven Borceg7 min readMarch 3, 2026

Texas, America's second-largest state, has a baby naming culture all its own — blending Southern tradition, Hispanic heritage, and a fiercely independent spirit.

Top 10 Baby Names in Texas (2026)

Girls

  • Olivia
  • Emma
  • Mia
  • Camila
  • Charlotte
  • Sofia
  • Isabella
  • Amelia
  • Luna
  • Harper

    • Boys

  • Liam
  • Noah
  • Mateo
  • Oliver
  • Sebastian
  • James
  • Lucas
  • Elijah
  • Benjamin
  • William

    • Texas-Specific Naming Trends

    The Cowboy Factor

    Names like Wyatt, Austin, Dallas, and Maverick rank higher in Texas than nationally.

    Hispanic Heritage

    With 40% Hispanic population, names like Santiago, Diego, Valentina, and Ximena are mainstream.

    Southern Tradition

    Double names (Mary Jane, John David) and family surnames as first names remain popular, especially in East Texas.

    Oil & Ranch Culture

    Strong, rugged names like Colton, Colt, Ryder, and Gunner are more popular in Texas than anywhere else.

    Urban vs. Rural Texas

    Austin and Houston show naming patterns similar to coastal cities, while rural Texas maintains traditional Southern naming conventions. Dallas falls somewhere in between.

    Check How Many People Have This Name
